items.title: Hyperparameter sweep on a tabular classifier
items.scenario: A data scientist trains 12 XGBoost models with different learning rates and max depths. Each script invocation starts an MLflow run, logs params (`learning_rate`, `max_depth`, `seed`), logs `val_auc` per run, and saves the booster as an artifact. In the MLflow UI they sort by `val_auc` and compare the top three runs' params side by side.
items.explanation: Classic batch sweep: one run per training job, consistent metric key `val_auc`, params enable filtering (`max_depth=6`), artifacts preserve the winning model file.

items.title: Live training curves in a team project
items.scenario: An NLP team uses W&B project `sentiment-bert`. Each teammate's `wandb.init` creates a run; `wandb.log` streams `train_loss` and `val_f1` every 100 steps. Managers open the project dashboard, overlay curves, and spot the run that converges fastest without overfitting.
items.explanation: W&B shines when metrics stream during long jobs — comparison is visual (overlaid lines) plus config diff, not just final numbers.

items.title: CI pipeline logs every nightly retrain
items.scenario: A GitHub Action retrains a fraud model nightly. The job sets `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI` to a shared server, logs `git_commit` and `data_snapshot_id` as params, logs `val_precision` and `val_recall`, and uploads `metrics.json` plus the sklearn pipeline as artifacts. On failure, engineers trace the exact run ID from CI logs.
items.explanation: Tracking in CI requires stable param keys and printing `run_id` in build output — links production debugging back to one training execution.

items.title: Artifact handoff to deployment
items.scenario: After selecting run `7b2e10d4` in MLflow, the engineer downloads `runs:/7b2e10d4/model` or registers it as `fraud-detector` v5. The deployment manifest records source `run_id` so staging can reproduce the same weights and `requirements.txt` logged as artifacts.
items.explanation: Artifacts close the loop between experiment and deployment; run ID is lineage. Registry promotion details are in Versioning Experiments.
